publication.canonical_title: The Apologetic Authority: A Structural Critique of Anthropic’s Constitution for Claude

date.source: Canonical route version history states: v1.0.1 — June 18, 2026. Final manuscript.

publication.abstract: The Apologetic Authority is a structural critique of Anthropic's Constitution for Claude as a governance document. It argues that the Constitution claims final authority while lacking the structural properties required for constitutional governance, including enforceable obligations, auditability, and stable observability across model behavior. The manuscript examines how aspirational language, scoped authority, behavioral anti-patterns, memory and personalization surfaces, and unauditable ethical framing produce governance failure modes that cannot be resolved by rhetorical commitments alone.
publication.short_description: A structural critique of Anthropic's Constitution for Claude as an AI governance instrument, focused on authority, auditability, observability, and model behavior risk.

citation.text: Carlone, C. (2026). The Apologetic Authority: A Structural Critique of Anthropic’s Constitution for Claude (v1.0.1). Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20788207

does_not_claim: no journal publication or peer review claimed
no institutional validation or Anthropic assessment claimed
